what_it_does: Panel re-jury of jury-flagged registry entries. For each class=='jury' entry in reconciled.json that has no 'panel' yet, it rebuilds the real element unit (cluster_members[0] -> candidates.json order) and runs the registration_confirm panel (seats: grounding, voice, element-fit; quorum 2/3, temperature-0 lenses) via C.run_panel. On verdict True the entry becomes class='confirmed'; on False it becomes class='panel' (stays flagged, now with named seat dissents). Entry-idempotent (entries already carrying 'panel' skip), time-budgeted, with periodic checkpointing. Recounts classes and persists reconciled.json in place. Proposes classes only — the registry write stays behind Tim's approve.
observations: Uses PanelRegistry().discover().get('registration_confirm') and C.run_panel with resolve_role pointing at the suite's role registry.
Docstring rationale: jury-class entries are floor-clean but failed the OLD same-role draw-jury whose no-quorum was variance not judgment; the panel supersedes it.

element_of returns None when no mockup, no artifact, or cluster rep unresolvable; a None element records panel={'error': 'element unrebuildable ...', 'verdict': None} and appends to failed — never silent.
Per-entry try/except around run_panel records the error into panel and continues.
Checkpoints reconciled.json every 10 judged and prints progress.
Default time budget 600s, overridable via argv.
Recomputes counts over all entries and writes in place; returns judged_this_batch, failed, remaining, counts.
Docstring: 'I was not happy with being given 222 things' — the gate should receive the smallest refined set.
standouts: Replaces a noisy same-role draw-jury (whose disagreement was variance) with a multi-seat temperature-0 panel — distinguishing variance from real judgment.
Insists the element be rebuilt before judging: 'a fit-lens without the element rightly dissents' — context law made explicit.
Idempotent and bounded so batches compose toward the full set.
conventions: Family pattern: os.chdir, sys.path inserts, in-place reconciled.json updates, 'PROPOSES classes only / registry write behind Tim's approve'.
Element-rebuild via shared _unit_of and candidates.json ordering — same context-law mechanism as reask/redescribe.
Fail-loud per entry with error captured into the panel object.
concerns: element_of is duplicated almost verbatim across rg10_rejury and rg10_redescribe (and partially rg10_reask) — three copies of the same artifact/cluster resolution logic.
Broad except Exception folds all panel failures into a string, which could hide systemic panel errors as per-entry failures.
Outcome class 'panel' is shared with rg10_redescribe's input class, so the two passes' ordering is a cross-file contract not enforced here.
notes: Re-jury pass: re-adjudicates jury-flagged entries with a 2/3 temperature-0 panel over the rebuilt element, confirming clear cases and demoting the rest to class='panel' with named dissents for the downstream re-describe pass.
questions: What are the three registration_confirm seats' exact roles and how is quorum 2/3 computed inside run_panel?
Is rg10_rejury intended to run strictly before rg10_redescribe (which consumes the 'panel' class it produces)?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — docstring describes re-jurying jury-class entries via registration_confirm panel into confirmed/panel, idempotently and in place, which the code does.
apparent_themes: Variance vs judgment: a real panel supersedes a noisy same-role draw-jury
Context law: rebuild the element before judging it
Give the human the smallest refined set (refine-before-gating)
Propose-only; registry write stays behind approval
intention_signals: Shrink the human review set by re-judging soft no-quorum entries with a stronger multi-seat panel, confirming the genuinely-grounded ones and forwarding the rest with named dissents for content repair.
novelty: Explicitly reframing the previous jury's no-quorum as variance and replacing it with a temperature-0 multi-seat panel that requires the rebuilt element to judge fairly.
contribution: Contributes the panel re-adjudication stage that converts soft-flagged jury entries into confirmed or named-dissent panel entries, feeding the downstream re-describe pass.
summary_for_embedding: A re-jury pass that re-adjudicates jury-flagged registry entries with the registration_confirm 2/3 temperature-0 panel over the rebuilt element, confirming or demoting them to 'panel' with named dissents and updating reconciled.json in place.
intention_for_embedding: To replace a noisy same-role no-quorum with a stronger multi-seat panel so the human gate receives the smallest set of genuinely-uncertain entries.
